name: Cornalvo Reservoir Loop
name_original: Presa de Cornalvo – circular por el Parque Natural de Cornalvo
description: Hike an easy 4.7-mile (7.5 km) circular route in Cornalvo Natural Park, featuring the historic Roman Cornalvo Dam and oak dehesas.
region_intro: Bus station hiking trails in Cornalvo traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, expansive oak dehesas, and small mountain ranges. The terrain includes granite boulders, meadows, and riparian vegetation along watercourses, supporting diverse habitats. Mediterranean forests thrive along mountain slopes, representing well-preserved ecosystems. A key feature is the historic Roman Cornalvo Dam and its reservoir, which is still in use today. Best bus station hiking trails in Cornalvo The most popular bus station hiking route is Capitoline She-Wolf of Mérida loop from Acueducto de los Milagros, a 5.5 miles (8.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 17 minutes to complete. This moderate route features historical aqueducts and views of the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hiking loop from Mérida, a moderate 7.6 miles (12.2 km) path. This trail offers varied terrain with gentle ascents and views of the region's natural features. Local hikers also love the Acueducto de los Milagros – Los Milagros Aqueduct loop from Mérida, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail leading through historical sites and natural areas, often completed in about 1 hour 23 minutes. Hiking in Cornalvo is defined by oak dehesas, Mediterranean forests, and historical Roman structures, with options for different ability levels. The routes in Cornalvo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 390 hikers have used komoot to explore Cornalvo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are accessible directly from bus stations in Cornalvo?
A: There are 3 dedicated hiking routes in Cornalvo that are easily accessible from bus stations, offering convenient starting points for your adventure.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hikes starting from bus stations in Cornalvo?
A: The bus-accessible hikes in Cornalvo range from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40349016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;easy&lt;/a&gt; to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40344531" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;moderate&lt;/a&gt;. This makes them suitable for a wide range of hikers, including beginners and those looking for a relaxed outing.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available from bus stations?
A: Yes, all the bus-accessible routes in this guide are circular. For example, you can enjoy the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40344531" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capitoline She-Wolf of Mérida loop from Acueducto de los Milagros&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40349016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acueducto de los Milagros – Los Milagros Aqueduct loop from Mérida&lt;/a&gt;, both bringing you back to your starting point.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see on these bus-accessible trails?
A: Many of these trails offer glimpses of the region's characteristic oak dehesas, with ancient holm oaks and cork oaks. You might also encounter riparian vegetation along watercourses and granite boulders. The park is known for its diverse habitats supporting over 250 vertebrate species.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks along the bus station hiking routes?
A: Absolutely. The routes often pass by significant historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40349016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acueducto de los Milagros – Los Milagros Aqueduct loop from Mérida&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the impressive Roman aqueduct. While not directly on these specific routes, the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2990429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Dam&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage site, is a key historical attraction in the wider park.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Cornalvo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.68 stars. Reviewers often praise the tranquil beauty of the Extremaduran landscape, the well-preserved natural environments, and the opportunity to combine nature with historical exploration.

Q: Are these bus-accessible trails suitable for families?
A: Yes, there are family-friendly options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40349016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acueducto de los Milagros – Los Milagros Aqueduct loop from Mérida&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.2 km route, making it a great choice for families with children. It offers a manageable distance and interesting sights.

Q: Can I bring my dog on these hiking trails?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in Cornalvo Natural Park, but it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock. Please ensure you follow local regulations and clean up after your pet.

Q: When is the best time of year to hike these bus-accessible trails in Cornalvo?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking in Cornalvo, offering mild temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Spring brings wildflowers, while autumn showcases beautiful foliage. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler and wetter.

Q: Are there any facilities like cafes or picnic areas near the bus station trailheads?
A: While direct facilities at every bus stop trailhead might be limited, the routes often start or pass near urban areas like Mérida where amenities are readily available. Within the wider park, there is a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6238086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic Area Near the Roman Bridge of Aljucén&lt;/a&gt;, and a visitor's center provides information on the park's features.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I spot on these trails?
A: Cornalvo Natural Park is rich in biodiversity. Keep an eye out for various bird species, especially around water features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3071953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The park is home to over 250 vertebrate species, including endangered ones like the Jarabugo fish and the black stork.
tips: Built around 130 AD, this dam was used to supply water to the ancient Roman city of Augusta Emerita. The reservoir and its surroundings were declared a natural park in 2004, making it an ideal destination for nature and history lovers.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: This easy circular trail is approximately 7.5 kilometers (4.7 miles) long and generally takes about 1 hour and 56 minutes to complete, making it a pleasant outing for most hikers.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Presa de Cornalvo circular trail is considered an "easy" hike with minimal elevation gain, making it suitable for all skill levels, including beginners and families.

faqs.question: Where is the trailhead and parking for this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Cornalvo Natural Park. Parking is typically available near the historic Cornalvo Dam, which serves as a common starting point for the circular route.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this circular trail?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses diverse landscapes, including expansive oak dehesas, rolling hills, and Mediterranean forests. You'll encounter granite boulders and lush riverine vegetation along the Aljucén River, with picturesque views of the historic Roman dam and the reservoir's unique, horn-like shape.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter in Cornalvo Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Cornalvo Natural Park is a haven for birdwatching. You might spot species like black storks, white storks, and honey buzzards. The park's rich biodiversity also supports other local wildlife.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. During these seasons, the weather is ideal for hiking, and the natural park is particularly beautiful.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The most significant feature is the historic Cornalvo Dam, a Roman engineering marvel still in use today and part of a UNESCO World Heritage site. You'll also see impressive cork oaks, including the notable 400-year-old "Emérito, el Abuelo de Cornalvo," and enjoy scenic views of the reservoir and dehesa landscapes.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Embalse de Cornalvo trail, which covers approximately 97% of this circular route.

faqs.question: Is the Presa de Cornalvo trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Cornalvo Natural Park generally welcomes dogs, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash to protect the local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in Cornalvo Natural Park?
faqs.answer: There are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking this trail within the Cornalvo Natural Park. However, it's always a good idea to check for any updated regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: Is there an interpretation center or facilities nearby?
faqs.answer: Yes, an interpretation center is located near the Cornalvo Dam. It provides valuable insights into the historical and natural significance of the site and the surrounding Cornalvo Natural Park.
